exploit_minted_token.role: qXETH (xETH wrapped) on BSC — the destination-chain token minted in the exploit. Used as collateral to drain Qubit lending pool.

root_cause_summary.vulnerable_pattern: QBridge.deposit treats token == address(0) as native sentinel but does not require msg.value == amount, so attackers emit arbitrary Deposit events with msg.value = 0.
root_cause_summary.verification_bypass: Cross-chain relayer + mint path trust the relayed (amount, recipient, nonce) tuple and mint qXETH without re-validating that the source actually transferred native ETH.
root_cause_summary.notes: The reconstruction in contracts/ models the bug across two routers (QBridgeETH + QBridgeBSC) for clarity even though the production deployment kept both legs in a single multi-network router. The deposit-side address was not publicly verified at the time of incident; the BSC mint-side router is the documented entry point on BscScan.

exploit_characteristics.notes: A single attacker transaction emitted a forged Deposit and minted ~206,809 qXETH on BSC, then drained over $80M of TVL via subsequent borrows.
